FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class SplitView

Namespace
FluentGpu.Controls
Assembly
FluentGpu.Controls.dll

A WinUI SplitView: a side pane and a flexible content area, composed per SplitViewDisplayMode × PanePlacement × IsPaneOpen (the WinUI visual-state table, SplitView_Partial.cpp:34-59):

<ul><li><b>Overlay</b> (the WinUI default) — the open pane floats over the content without reflow (PaneRoot
Canvas.ZIndex=1 over a ColumnSpan=2 ContentRoot, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:700, :718) and light-dismisses on
content click / Escape / a host resize, with the WinUI 0.35s/0.12s slide.</li><li><b>Inline</b> — the open pane reserves <code>paneWidth</code> of layout; the content pre-slides in sync
(ContentTransform, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:345-348) over the 0.2s/0.1s inline curves.</li><li><b>CompactOverlay / CompactInline</b> — closed leaves a <code>compactPaneLength</code> (48) rail: the SAME pane,
clipped to the rail (a clip-reveal, never a resize — core SplitView.cpp:343-377).</li></ul>

State is caller-owned (<code class="paramref">isPaneOpen</code> is a prop, the WinUI <code>IsPaneOpen</code> DP): wire
<code class="paramref">setIsPaneOpen</code> so light dismiss can write the close back. PaneOpening/PaneOpened/
PaneClosing(cancelable on light dismiss)/PaneClosed mirror the WinUI events (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:82-96).

public static class SplitView

object
SplitView

object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Fields

PartContent

The flexible content area (WinUI ContentRoot). Owned: Animate (the inline content pre-slide), Children (the content slot).

public const string PartContent = "Content"

Field Value

string

PartPane

The pane surface (WinUI PaneRoot's inner Border) — mounted while the pane is open OR while a compact rail shows. Owned: Width (always the full OpenPaneLength — the compact rail CLIPS it), OnRealized (the focus-contract handle capture, chained), Children (the pane content slot).

public const string PartPane = "Pane"

Field Value

string

Methods

Create(Element, Element, float, bool, SplitViewDisplayMode, SplitViewPanePlacement, float, Action<bool>?, Action?, Action?, Action<SplitViewPaneClosingArgs>?, Action?, TemplateParts?) 

public static Element Create(Element pane, Element content, float paneWidth = 320, bool isPaneOpen = false, SplitViewDisplayMode displayMode = SplitViewDisplayMode.Overlay, SplitViewPanePlacement panePlacement = SplitViewPanePlacement.Left, float compactPaneLength = 48, Action<bool>? setIsPaneOpen = null, Action? onPaneOpening = null, Action? onPaneOpened = null, Action<SplitViewPaneClosingArgs>? onPaneClosing = null, Action? onPaneClosed = null, TemplateParts? parts = null)

Parameters

pane Element

The pane content (WinUI SplitView.Pane).

content Element

The main content (WinUI SplitView.Content).

paneWidth float

WinUI OpenPaneLength — default 320 (SplitViewOpenPaneThemeLength, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:5).

isPaneOpen bool

WinUI IsPaneOpen — CLR default false (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:58-60; PaneRoot starts Collapsed, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:700).

displayMode SplitViewDisplayMode

Default Overlay (the WinUI DP default — SplitView_Partial.cpp:136, :446).

panePlacement SplitViewPanePlacement
compactPaneLength float

WinUI CompactPaneLength — default 48 (SplitViewCompactPaneThemeLength, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:6).

setIsPaneOpen Action<bool>

Write-back for the caller-owned open state — light dismiss (content click, Escape, host resize) closes through this. Null ⇒ light dismiss is inert (state cannot be flipped).

onPaneOpening Action
onPaneOpened Action
onPaneClosing Action<SplitViewPaneClosingArgs>

Cancelable on light dismiss only; an app-initiated close (the caller flipping isPaneOpen) raises it but ignores Cancel (core SplitView.cpp:432-456).

onPaneClosed Action
parts TemplateParts

Returns

Element
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
